publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-07-22 18:15:33 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes correspondence concerning the graduate program of the New York State Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva, 1940-1983; selection of a head for the Entomology Department at Cornell University, 1965; and Rachel Carson's SILENT SPRING, 1962-1963. Also, research material concerning SILENT SPRING.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

relatedmaterial.head: NOTES
relatedmaterial.p: Related collection:Geneva Experiment Station Project. Oral History Interview with Paul Chapman, #13-6-944, trsc.3888.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Materials on Rachel Carson's Silent Spring Box 1 folders1-5
Series IV. National Science Foundation Grants Box 1 folders 19-23
Series V. Annual Review Articles Box 1 folders 24-29
Series VI. Articles, Papers, etc. Box 1 folders 30-49 Box 2 folders 1-16
Series VII. Station History Publication Box 2 folders 17-30
Series VIII. Photo Album- "P. J. Chapman, The First 68 Years" Box 3
Series XI. Oral History Interviews. Personal Box 4 folders 11
Series XIII. Subject Files Box 4 folders 16-31

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Paul Jones Chapman papers, #22-1-2102. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence concerning the graduate program of the New York State Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva, 1940-1983; selection of a head for the Entomology Department at Cornell University, 1965; and Rachel Carson's SILENT SPRING, 1962-1963. Also, research material concerning SILENT SPRING, the experiment stations, grant files, annual review articles, and photographs.

controlaccess.subject: Entomology -- Study and teaching.
controlaccess.persname: Carson, Rachel, 1907-1964. (Title of work: Silent spring..)
controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Agriculture. Department of Entomology
New York State Agricultural Experiment Station

USSR- Europa Caucasus, Tashkent and Alma Ata
Seed treatment for the control of fungus diseases and the seed corn maggot
"Should Chemists Conducting Chemical Research in the Entomology Field Be Assigned to a Chemistry or Entomology Division?"

Dr. Dean's manuscript on Codling Moth
The European Chafer-Glasgow and later correspondence
The European Chafer-Reports , Authorized Control Measures
Dr. Marvin Harris, Materials on Pecans (Carya)
The Problems Encountered in Dividing Up the Field of Plant Agriculture Between Cornell's Two Agricultural Experiment Stations
Seeds and Vegetable Sciences, Chapter XVI

A Brief History of the ASE, etc.
Working Relationship Between NYSAES at Geneva and Research Programs on Cornell Campus, Ithaca-Tony Shelton
History of New York State Agricultural Experiment Station (NYSAES)
Drafts for Hatch Act Centennial; Centennial of NYSAES; Department of Entomology (Geneva and Ithaca)
History of Parrott Hall and the Octogon House; Gov. Flowers Visits Geneva; Geneva Historical Society and Museum
Station History, Misc. Writing by Chapman, Glass and Krauss
Misc. Early and Late Drafts of Chapman's Early Chapters

Transcription of E. H. Glass Seminar Presented June 24, 1982
Series VIII: Photo Album "P. J. Chapman, The First 68 Years"
Correspondence concerning Chief of Research at Geneva
